content.full_text:
RAvI MENTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
September 2, 1997
Michael Biddle, City Attorney
City ofEmeryville
Office ofthe City Attorney
2200 Powell Street, 12th Floor
Emeryville, California 94608
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-97-413
Dear Mr. Biddlle:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the “Act”).’
QUESTION
Does the term “public board or commission,” as used by Section 87505, include the City
ofEmeryville’s civic center project committee, the housing committee, andJor the finance
committee?
CONCLUSION
The housing committee is a “public board or commission”; thus, and its appointed
members are subject to the prohibitions of Section 85705. The civic center project committee
and the finance committee are not currently a “public board or commission”; thus, their
appointed members are not subject to the prohibitions of Section 85705.
FACTS
The City ofEmeryville has a large number ofboards, commissions, and committees that
were established by the city council to provide advice on specific issues. Some ofthe
committees are standing committees that handle ongoing matters, such as the housing committee
and others are temporary committees that handle single projects, such as the civic center project
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 -
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
428 JSTREwr, Surrn8QO. SACRAMENTO.CAL1FnRNTAQSR1A
(Q1 39-c6n


File No. A-97-413
Page 2
committee. Some ofthe committees are subcommittees ofthe city council and are comprised of
two members ofthe city council. These committees are advisory only. The city council makes
an independent judgment on the issue based on an independent review ofthe facts and
information available, ofwhich the committee’s recommendation is one element.
The first committee is the civic center project committee. The purpose ofthe committee
is to provide recommendations to the city council on the design ofthe new city hall. The
committee has made two recommendations over the past three months: the selection of an
architect and the use of an adjoining property for a new building. The council approved the
recommended architect and the use ofthe adjoining property. The council directed staffto
proceed with the adjoining property and provide staffwith additional directions as to security and
other design issues which were not part ofthe committee’s recommendations.
The second committee is the housing committee. This is a standing committee that
reviews proposed housing projects and provides recommendations to the planning commission
and the city council. It was formed in 1990 and has made numerous recommendations to the
council over the years. The recommendations do not include whether the project should be
approved, but how to proceed with the development ofthe project. The council does not always
go through the housing committee for decisions on projects. Ifthe housing committee makes a
recommendation, the city council debates the issue on the merits, considering all information
including the recommendation of the housing committee. It is the general impression of the staff
without a thorough review ofthe record, that the council follows the recommendations ofthe
housing committee in approximately 65 to 75 percent of the cases.
The third committee is the finance committee. It consists oftwo members of the city
council who are appointed by the full council. The purpose ofthe committee is to review finance
issues and provide advice to the full council as appropriate. This committee has only one regular
meeting per year.
The staffs impression is that this committee’s recommendations are followed
approximately 50 percent ofthe time.
APPLICABLE LAW
Section 85705, which was added by Proposition 208, states as follows:
“No person appointed to a public board or commission or as
Trustee ofthe California State University or Regent ofthe
University of California during tenure in office shall donate to, or
solicit or accept any campaign contribution for, any committee
controlled by the person who made the appointment to that office
or any other entity with the intent that the recipient ofthe donation
be any committee controlled by such person who made the
appointment.”


File No. A-97-413
Page 3
The Act does not define “public board” or “commission.” However, Commission staff
has developed guidelines for when a governmental entity is a “public board” or “commission”
for purposes of Section 85705. Commission staffhas concluded that any appointed board or
similar body created by a governmental entity and empowered (formally or informally) to make
governmental decisions is a “public” body within the meaning of Section 85705. (Lyions Advice
Letter, No. A-97-203.)
To determine whether a board or similar body is authorized to make a governmental
decision, we look to Regulation 18700(a)(l):
“A board or commission possesses decisionmaking authority
whenever:
(A) It may make a final governmental decision.
(B) It may compel a governmental decision; or it may prevent a
governmental decision either by reason of an exclusive power to
initiate the decision or by reason of a veto which may not be
overridden; or
(C) It makes substantive recommendations which are, and over an
extended period oftime have been, regularly approved without
significant amendment or modification by another public official
or governmental agency.”
ANALYSIS
You have asked whether a number ofthe City ofEmeryville’s advisory committees
satisfy the criteria in Regulation 1 8700(a)(l)(C).
Civic Center Project Committee
This committee was established for what will be a limited time for a limited purpose, to
provide recommendations to the city council on the design ofthe new city hall. To date the
committee has existed for three months. Commission staffhas consistently held that committees
established for a limited time for a limited purpose are not the type ofbody contemplated in
Regulation 18700(a)(l)(C). (Wasserman Advice Letter, No. A-97-153; Biddle Advice Letter,
No. A-93-390.) Consequently, the prohibitions of Section 85705 do not apply to members of the
civic center project committee. However, if in the future, the committee should continue to exist
even after it has fulfilled the limited purpose for which it was created, and its substantive
recommendations continue to be approved without significant amendment or modification by the
city council, then the committee would fall within the prohibitions of Section 85705.
Housing Committee
The housing committee is a standing committee that has been in existence since 1990 and
has made numerous recommendations to the city council and the planning commission. You


File No. A-97-413
Page 4
have stated that the city council follows the recommendations ofthe housing committee in
approximately 65 to 75 percent ofthe cases. However, you have not stated whether the city
council or planning commission follows the recommendation ofthe housing committee “without
significant amendment or modification” when they do follow the recommendations ofthe
housing committee. If it is the case that the recommendations are followed without significant
amendment or modification in approximately 65 to 75 percent ofthe cases, then the housing
committee meets the requirements ofRegulation l8700(a)(l)(C). Therefore, the prohibitions of
Section 85705 apply to appointed members ofthe housing committee.
Finance Committee
It is unclear from the facts provided whether the finance committee is a standing
committee or how long it has been in existence. However, you have stated that the finance
committee has only one regular meeting a year and that its recommendations are followed
approximately 50 percent ofthe time by the city council. Again, you do no state whether the
recommendations are made “without significant amendment or modification.” Nevertheless,
Commission staff finds that a committee whose recommendations are followed approximately 50
percent ofthe time is not a committee whose recommendations are “regularly approved.”
Therefore, the prohibitions of Section 85705 do not apply to members of the finance committee.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-
5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

2
By:
Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
